column1: Forensic / Quality-of-Earnings checklist — run on any company. (Companion to RED-FLAGS-SATYAM.md.) A flag = a QUESTION to investigate

column3: Interest income / average cash & bank balance (implied yield)
column4: Implied yield far below bank/FD rates — cash may not be real or is oddly idle

column3: 5-yr Net profit vs Operating cash flow
column4: Profits rise but operating cash flow lags persistently
column5: Reported profits had no matching real cash

column3: Days Sales Outstanding (DSO) vs peers and own history
column4: DSO drifting up vs peers — possible fictitious/aggressive revenue

column3: Size & direction of related-party deals in the notes
column4: Shareholder cash flowing toward promoter/insiders without clear logic
column5: Maytas: tried to buy promoter's sons' firms with the cash

column3: Trend in promoter holding and pledged shares
column4: Promoter selling/pledging while reporting record results
column5: Promoter stake fell over the fraud years

column3: Tenure / fees / resignations / qualifications; do they confirm bank balances
column4: Long unchanged auditor; sudden resignation; balances not independently confirmed
column5: Auditors did not independently verify the cash

column3: Operating & net margin vs close competitors
column4: Margins materially better than peers with no clear reason

column3: Share of profit from non-core / other income
column4: Profit propped up by non-operating items

column3: Off-balance-sheet items and commitments in the notes
column4: Large or growing relative to net worth

column2: Start with the cash. Real money earns a yield and gets returned to owners. If the cash doesn't behave like real money, ask why before you trust anything else.
